steps.title: Find the slope of line s
steps.content:
Rearrange $8x + 2y = 0$ to slope-intercept form:
$$2y = -8x$$
$$y = -4x$$
The slope of line $s$ is $-4$.

steps.title: Find the slope of line t using the perpendicular rule
steps.content:
Since line $t$ is perpendicular to line $s$, its slope is the negative reciprocal of $-4$:
$$m_t = -\frac{1}{-4} = \frac{1}{4}$$

steps.title: Use the two points on line t to find k
steps.content:
Line $t$ passes through $(6, 0)$ and $(k, \frac{5}{4})$ with slope $\frac{1}{4}$. Using the slope formula:
$$\frac{\frac{5}{4} - 0}{k - 6} = \frac{1}{4}$$
Cross-multiply: $\frac{5}{4} \cdot 4 = k - 6$, so $5 = k - 6$. Therefore $k = 11$.
